Entries.Name: Background Apps
Entries.Detail: Controls whether apps are allowed to run and update in the background when they are not in focus. Disabling saves CPU and memory.
Entries.CautionReason: May prevent automatic updates. Disabling Background Apps stops all apps from running in the background, breaking push notifications, live tiles, email sync, and automatic app updates from the Microsoft Store.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When disabled, UWP apps do not receive updates, notifications, or trigger background tasks while not in the foreground. Live tile data and push notifications will stop refreshing.

Entries.Name: Copilot App
Entries.Description: Install or remove Copilot, Recall, and Windows AI components
Entries.Detail: Uninstall removes: Copilot app and Store package, Copilot in Windows Search, Copilot sidebar in Edge, Image Creator in Paint, Recall and its scheduled tasks, AI voice effects, App Actions, WSAIFabric and Agent Activation services, .copilot file extension, AI Appx packages, and related registry policies. Install attempts to restore the Copilot Store app via winget or Microsoft Store.
Entries.CautionReason: May require Store or winget to recover. Uninstall removes Copilot, Recall, AI services, and related packages. Restoring requires winget or Microsoft Store access, a supported Windows build, and manual package recovery steps.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Removes Copilot, Recall, and the related Windows AI component packages. Reinstallation requires winget or Microsoft Store access.

Entries.Name: Cortana Autostart
Entries.Detail: Prevents Cortana from launching automatically at startup. Cortana can still be opened manually if needed.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Prevents Cortana from registering a startup entry. Cortana can still be launched manually; this only stops it from starting automatically with Windows.

Entries.Name: Edge Debloat
Entries.Detail: Applies Group Policy settings to disable telemetry, shopping assistant, collections, Copilot sidebar, and other optional Edge extras.
Entries.CautionReason: May require Store or winget to recover. Enforces system-wide Group Policy settings that disable Edge telemetry, personalization, shopping, collections, rewards, and Copilot sidebar. Reverting requires clearing the applied policies manually.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Edge debloat policies are not applied
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Writes Group Policy values to disable Edge telemetry, the shopping assistant, Collections, the Copilot sidebar, and other optional features. Does not affect browsing, sync, or extensions.

Entries.Name: New Outlook
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the new Outlook app is available. Disable to keep using the classic Mail and Calendar apps instead.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Removes or disables the new Outlook (formerly Mail & Calendar) app. Disabling reverts to the classic Win32 Outlook or Mail app.

Entries.Name: Notifications
Entries.Detail: Master switch for the entire Windows notification system. Disabling stops all app notifications and hides the notification center.
Entries.CautionReason: May prevent automatic updates. Disabling Notifications completely turns off Windows notifications. You will not receive app alerts, system security warnings, update reminders, calendar events, or two-factor authentication prompts. The notification tray and calendar flyout will not function.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Disables the entire Windows notification system. No app will be able to deliver toasts or badge updates, and the Action Center will be empty.

Entries.Name: Revert Start Menu (24H2)
Entries.Description: Revert to original Start Menu from 24H2
Entries.Detail: Reverts the Start Menu to the pre-24H2 layout on Windows 11 24H2 builds. May affect compatibility with future Windows updates.
Entries.CautionReason: May prevent automatic updates. Reverting the Start Menu to pre-24H2 layout may break future Windows cumulative updates that depend on the new Start Menu components, requiring additional tooling or a clean install to recover.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: New Start Menu is shown (not reverted)
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Rolls back the Start Menu layout introduced in Windows 11 24H2. Applied via a registry key that may be reset by future Windows updates.

Entries.Name: UWP Apps (Bulk)
Entries.Description: Bulk install or uninstall selected Windows Store apps
Entries.Detail: Opens a selection dialog to remove or restore selected Windows Store apps. This is package management rather than a simple toggle, and some removals cannot be reversed automatically.
Entries.CautionReason: May require Store or winget to recover. Uninstall removes selected Windows app packages, not just UI shortcuts. Some apps cannot be restored from the Microsoft Store once removed, and recovery may require winget, DISM, or a Windows repair install. Excluded from removal: Edge, Windows Store, Terminal, Notepad, WSL, media codecs, and drivers.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Opens a selection dialog to remove or restore Store apps in bulk. Some removals are one-way or require winget, Microsoft Store, or manual reinstall to reverse.
